dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Normally, sweet Aphrodite's power over beauty provides no shortage of entertainment I'm sure, though I fail to see how her blessing can be of much benefit in such a predicament as ours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your fair cousin Apollo wields the light that mortals so cherish, for it brings them warmth, melting away my snow. With my permission, of course! His power shall only help you so much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew warlike Ares would be unable to resist giving our present conflict his personal attention. Although I doubt he shall help put it to an end, for he is thoroughly enjoying this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps I misjudged your woodsy cousin Artemis. To think that she found you! I was certain she was merely gallivanting with her Nymph-friends rather than being of use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Did the famous wisdom of Athena live up to her reputation, flower? I would think our master strategist should not have to defend the front lines herself. But what do I know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I've so much I would speak with you about, Melinoë... but this is not the hour or the means. At least you can hear me, can't you? And I can sense your presence, faint and distant though it may be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense the mark of Chaos is with you... so even such an ancient and uncaring power as that one has expressed concern for these events in which we find ourselves? Perhaps merely an interest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I detested Chronos even in my youth. Almost as much as my own Titan father. We took our vengeance, though; on Chronos, Hyperion, and the rest. But I was a fool to think we'd never see any of them again...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: We've been locked in conflict with Chronos for so long that we've begun to doubt that anything can meaningfully change. I wonder, flower... can you truly rid us of this blight?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I take it Chronos still goes everywhere with that old gaudy-looking golden scythe of his? He fancies himself a reaper, speaks of harvest and decay; but he merely speaks in riddles, whilst I work directly in the trade.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: If it is true you reached the Underworld's depths and slew that wretched Titan there, then good. It likely shall take more than that, I know, but you must have inflicted some amount of pain, and perhaps fear. Which is a start.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You bested Chronos again didn't you, flower? We always knew he could be beaten; I once saw to it myself. But can he be destroyed? The Witch of the Crossroads must know...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The witch Circe, whom you know. She's distant daughter to my Titan-brother Helios the Sun, who dares not show his face. Her enthusiasm for soil and grain is almost cloying... though I appreciate the underlying sentiment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Last I checked, that drunkard Dionysus had refused to take up arms against his family, as though Chronos qualifies. I ought to sour all his crops...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Fear not for this mountaintop, nor for the Earth; for she is far more resilient than us all. Our foes could scour every trace of our existence, yet Mother Gaia would remain, and recover.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing lives in the city of Ephyra. And I shall keep it that way until its infestation of shambling wretches is brought to heel! Though, you need not concern yourself with the details; we have someone already on the case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Are you on your way here to us, flower? A journey that your mother made many a spring... but at a different time, whilst not beset by thuggish followers of a despicable Titan.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: How revolting to think that Chronos is technically a grandfather to you, as I am a grandmother. We have no connection at all, yet this family tree of ours has grown for many aeons and has twisted limbs by now...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You have one grandfather in the cruel Titan, Chronos... and another who was nothing but a mortal farm-boy worshiper of mine. Whether I loved or pitied him, I cannot say. At least your mother was born deathless...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Now even the weavers of destiny apparently are gone, and shall do nothing to preserve their stake in our futures? That is the talk on the mountain, Granddaughter. So then we truly are alone... but we can fend for ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Chronos claims not even he knows where the Three Fates currently reside, despite having cast them away himself? I've seldom heard such foolishness. To think such a bumbling fool could have come so close to overthrowing us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your journey sends you through the Mourning Fields. I've seen that place... just dreadful, utterly! All of the wheat there, it... I thought perhaps it would provide a bit of cheer...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father was sullen and difficult even whilst young, quite honestly. What my daughter saw in him, I could not. Yet my Kore as I called her... she would brighten around him...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Word reaches us that you located Hades, flower! How he must seethe, incarcerated in that pit of despair, although at least he's whole. What of your mother, nothing still...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Surely he would not admit to this... but your father has been through quite an ordeal. Worse perhaps than any of us. But he and your mother have weathered difficult times before, and each time they have grown from it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: A glint of something there... a Jeweled Pom, from your mother? She brought me one much like it as a gift when she was traveling here from the Underworld. Keep it well, flower; and may she always be with us, in turn.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The first time your mother was lost to me... the Witch of the Crossroads emerged from the shadows to light my way, as I searched to the ends of the Earth. We found not a thing; but at least I was not alone...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You know I always felt a kinship with Hecate, seldom though we saw each other. She never seemed to want for children of her own. But the Fates delivered you to her anyhow.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I reached out to the Witch of the Crossroads recently... to give thanks to her, for making such a difficult decision to keep you hidden for as long as she did. I know she did it for your safety... and to raise the likelihood we would prevail.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: That artifact you bear... such cold is emanating from it that I sense its power all the way from here. Whatever is the origin of all that deathlike frost, it isn't of this realm...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, you even got the elusive god of the forge to crawl out of his cave. To think that all it took was this terrible in-fighting with our forebear to bring our family closer together.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Well it seems you've earned the good graces of my sister Hera! And you didn't even have to commit yourself to a mate or bear any children, I suppose. She must be growing soft!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You're making haste, flower! I'd swear that you were graced by swift Hermes himself, except he must be too busy keeping watch over our enemies to be of much benefit to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: So Hestia gave you her gift of flame? Normally the old miser wants nothing to do with any of us, not even her sisters. But extraordinary times do call for extraordinary measures.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: We are connected not unlike that Sheaf of Barley which you have. Each stem is broken easily, and yet the bunch is strong! Each different, and yet all very much alike.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: See how the Sheaf of Barley that I offered you has grown! Becoming stronger from your attention and care. Our relationships are much the same... a bit of truth that's far too easy to forget.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Hera and Zeus give far too much consideration to a plot that could potentially stop Typhon but would certainly bring ruin to our mountain in the attempt. Madness born of desperation! We mustn't take such reckless risks.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë... I was certain you were gone. Scarce born, only to be taken by Chronos, along with my daughter and those nearest her. But the Witch of the Crossroads got you out of there, did she? How could she not send word...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: When bright Apollo gathered us and announced that his twin sister, whilst out hunting for our enemies, stumbled upon a long-lost relative with the Witch of the Crossroads, why... I was overjoyed and furious at once...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You could have informed us; informed me, whilst you grew supple and strong in the Witch's care. Yet knowing Hecate... she must have deemed the risk too great. I understand. I'll try to understand...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Even in the dark of a new moon, when Selene herself dares not ride across the cold night sky, I found you there... and I shall give you all the power that I have to spare.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You have many of those little seedlings... each one a mystery. So minuscule, yet with potential to become a thing of beauty and power... in other words, to live. I know you shall nurture them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you doing in those brackish depths, flower? Even Poseidon has turned his back on what lurks in that corner of the sea. Does such a path truly lead deeper into hell?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Something about this night is troubling... as though some force of will yawned forth from the very depths of Chaos. But you wouldn't know anything of this, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: How strange that you are already this far along your nightly journey, flower, yet... I had a sense that I might find you here nevertheless. Perhaps from the connection that we share, or other forces now at play.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: There has been little cause for joy upon Olympus in the recent past, certainly not for me. But seeing you, flower? That made up for much of the hardship. I know it may be some time before we meet again, but also know we shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You inherited your mother's flaxen hair, and seemingly her force of will. Greenery would burst to life for her... the dead would too, if but they could. Within the reaches of my heart not yet grown cold, I know she still is safe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother used to visit us for months on end, but now... knowing that she is all right and restored to her Underworld throne, I could not ask for more. Not right away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Some sort of skull you have... one of the Arms of Night, I know, and yet, that form... something about it, it... reminds me not of Nyx, but... of my daughter? Ah, but do not listen to my ravings. Carry on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Most never even would have noticed all the many different plants you've found, but you possess a gift. In your care, they serve a greater purpose than merely to live; though to live is an achievement in itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I wonder if the vile pomegranate fruit yet grows in your Underworld. It holds a power there that I do not abide; a mere taste of its blood-like seeds can be very dangerous, indeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, you bear the blessing of Poseidon! Surely such a loud and blustering god must have more than sufficient might to back it up?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: So Poseidon has again availed himself to you! Well, good, and may he prove of use. I'll hold my threat to freeze his oceans then, for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Prometheus stole fire from us not for mortalkind, but because he desired it for his own gain. What good is that power to him now, upon a mountain cold as ice?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I could snuff out the flames of that Prometheus... but in so doing, all of mortalkind would likely freeze. I am not yet prepared to go that far, though likely can be pushed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: This scheme that Chronos has devised to keep all of us occupied... even Prometheus remains a nuisance as before! Inform us right away if you suspect any deceit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: That rainfall there may be of benefit to you; washing all the filth away, revealing new growth. Think you that Zeus himself brings the rain, or Poseidon? Take one more guess...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The bounties of the Earth... life flourishes even there where you reside, amid all that death. Flowers, roots, herbs, and seeds; you know their hidden strength. I let them grow for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You've picked an abundance of Moly flowers... unassuming little things, aren't they? Not unlike you, for they too hold power far beyond that suggested by their form.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You have in your possession Mandrake Root? Its poison if not its scream can end a mortal's life, though you and your Witch of the Crossroads must have learned to tame those properties...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Of late I feel increasingly at loss for words, flower. After all this toil... I may wilt from social graces for a while. Though you'll have my undying support for as long as you require.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: There's a touch of moonlight about you, flower, as though Selene lights your way. Would that the Moon concerned herself with more than her path across the sky...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a foreboding night this is! The air is thick with the death and decay of your father's realm, and the desolation I have wrought. The pale Moon can scarce cut through...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Something is amiss tonight... a foreboding that lingers in the air. I hesitate to speculate about what makes our foes all fly into a rage. But I would not be much surprised if it originated from your realm, not this one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Hera, Hestia, and I were far enough afield in disposition that we were not particularly close as sisters go. Although warring against Chronos has brought us somewhat closer, I suppose...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My sisters each have grown quite fond of you, flower. A rare source of consensus! Hestia, she's not as difficult compared to most, but Hera? Zeus himself would admit that our Queen is not easily impressed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Do you see those tiny flecks that drift down from the heavens to the Earth? I call it snow, and it is beautiful but very cold. We gods ought to be like the snow ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My understanding was your line could scarce survive beyond the reaches of the Underworld. Yet there you are, traipsing about as though not bound by your father's fate. How can this be...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You sprang forth from underneath that gloom unto our world's surface, have you, flower? If you mean to visit our mountain, merely follow the path of desolation leading straight to us...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The very ground beneath our feet has started to shift, and it bodes ill, I think. What does blasted Chronos now intend to do, topple our entire mountain to the Earth?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your recent descent took you deeper than my deepest roots, close to where the Titan Chronos now resides... and holds your mother captive with the rest. Is there no way to inform us of what happened?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your uncle Poseidon created that watery chasm you see before you. I've half a mind to freeze it shut, though I fear our enemies shall cross it anyhow, and your voyage would only be more fraught.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: That Rift of Thessaly could easily be turned to ice, you know. I'd like to see the enemy's fleet sail across it then! But I am told we have to cool it with the cataclysms for a while... mortals and their begging.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The incursion by that abhorrent monster Typhon... it is more than a threat, it is an insult! I control the seasons, yet he dares smother our mountain in his foul storm?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: If Chronos truly is behind the reawakening of Typhon, then he is not merely arrogant, but mad. He promises another Golden Age yet visits such a creature on this world?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Snow falls gently on Olympus as before, but no trace of Typhon's foul storm remains... or of the rest of him. But you are able to confront him still somewhere beyond...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Now the Titan Chronos is our solitary goal. Show him no more mercy than the monster he unleashed would have shown us. But be careful; I demand your safe return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: As much as I relished our victory over Typhon, to have finally met you meant the world to me, flower. Forgive that I am not known for my cheer, especially as of late. And may we see each other again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Zeus says you substantially contributed to Typhon's defeat at our summit. Thank you for that, flower, but I regret I could not have been there to say so at our Palace grounds. Our enemies are numerous, and not letting up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: That monster Typhon apparently has not yet understood that neither he nor Chronos shall stop us. But we shall make him, and your presence here may be the difference we need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: A proper garden must be carefully maintained. So it shall be with your Underworld as I see it, flower. If there exists some lingering influence from Chronos, let us root it out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I once visited the Underworld for an extended stay, you know. There in the House in which your parents live! We seldom spoke about it afterwards, but now... it mostly is a pleasant memory. Perhaps we all may try again sometime.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I miss your mother terribly, and your brother as well. He was my little sprout. Chronos must have ensnared him with the others in your father's House, the unforgivable wretch...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your brother Zagreus, why I suppose he's no longer my little sprout by now, grown as he has into a noble Underworld Prince. That you two finally know each other gladdens my old heart.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The great and crafty Zeus is aiding you directly, now? Then you truly must be one of our best remaining chances to thwart the Titan's plans... for Zeus is loath to ask for help, or offer it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I care very little for surprises, flower. And perhaps in that we are alike. Yet there exists a rare variety that's welcome... like a brisk breeze on an autumn eve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: How humiliating, that even our Boons must be concealed... lest we risk revealing too much about our plans and defenses to our enemies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Cold vengeance grows deep inside our hearts; let it flourish for a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I have slain Titans once or twice before; you get to do the honors now, it seems.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not rest until your mother the Queen is restored to her rightful throne.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: May Chronos shiver, knowing well his actions shall not go unpunished.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: My blessings, Granddaughter. You're like a cool winter's breeze.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You have your mother's strength. Take mine as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you feel the bitter cold even from there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: We are amid a dark season indeed, my granddaughter...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Desolation unto those who side with the Titans against us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: However I can aid in your pursuits, I shall, flower.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Can you feel the biting chill in the air, Granddaughter? It shall intensify for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: So many traitors stand against us now, but soon they all shall lie in ruin and decay.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Through the seasons I bring life as well as death; which shall it be this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Even the ichor flowing through a Titan's veins can be made to run cold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The days grow dark, though perhaps they shall take a turn for the better as yet...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: For mortals, life is precious as it is fleeting. We goddesses can snuff it out so easily...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You grow ever-stronger, flower, and it warms my cold heart.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Let none stand between you and your ambitions, Granddaughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Death and decay to the enemies of Olympus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Those who dare stand against Olympus shall be summarily destroyed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Pride and wrath run deep within our family, Granddaughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: It matters not that Chronos is our kin; there is no bond that cannot fray.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: May you know times of peace as you grow long in years, but for right now...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Be as complete and as relentless as the winter's cold, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, the ever-turning cycle of death and rebirth! Isn't it exquisite, in a way?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that the surface offered fairer climes, flower, but no; not yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Together let us see to it that Chronos reaps all that he has sown.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Grow strong and flourish like I know you can, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I think by now our foes are good and ripe for the harvesting.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Look what I picked just for you this eve, flower.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Tonight calls for inclement weather, so our enemies had better bundle up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: As you are my granddaughter, you are welcome to reap the benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The Earth shall drink deep of the blood of our enemies; those that can bleed, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: It remains unseasonably cold up here, flower. But you seem not to mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not hesitate to purge what warmth remains within the Earth, if that is what it takes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: None know better than I of just how cruel the turning of the seasons can be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall drain all remaining life from whichever of our enemies have any of it left.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Even in the wake of such devastation as we have seen, life can survive, and regrow.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: From the ashes of this war, may we yet flourish with sufficient time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Time threatened to take everything away from us, but we held fast, and always shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: This world has had enough of endless warring between Titans and gods...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: May your efforts underneath the Earth always lead you to fruitful results.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: That realm of yours, so utterly devoid of life... it is not too dissimilar from winters here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: This night is to be cold and dark... but you have fought through worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Seasons may take turns for the worse, but beyond there always lies a certain hope.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: So long as you trust Chronos to continue doing his part, I shall tolerate him; nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Death and decay, everywhere... the surface and the Underworld have grown so much alike.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You've so much of this world left to see, Granddaughter; and some of it is beautiful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The essence of life is the struggle to survive. Even we gods are not above it all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The warmth of spring shall come again eventually, although perhaps not soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress told me so much about you, and now at last we meet. In the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You look just like your mother. I'll not lose her again. I shall freeze hell in its entirety if that is what it takes to get her back. You'll help me, won't you, Granddaughter?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To think the goddess of seasons herself is my grandmother. I am honored to have made your acquaintance!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I see the Witch of the Crossroads taught you not just strength but grace! I hereby accept your offering, and have something for you as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Demeter! I'm grateful for your strength. This is hardly fitting in exchange, but please accept it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems there are such youth today as still can show my generation due respect. I am pleased my own granddaughter is such an example.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To the goddess whose power truly is the lifeblood of our world! It's by your grace that such an offering of Nectar may exist at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, flower, surely there are other matters weighing heavily upon you now, more so than honoring your elders in the customary way? Nevertheless, I'm grateful for this, truly.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Grandmother, we've so much making up to do for all the time we could have spent getting to know each other. I trust we'll do just that soon enough, though for right now, there's this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: It's strange... as much as I loved your mother, she never honored me thus. Perhaps I was too lenient with her, or perhaps your generation is merely more considerate of their predecessors.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I cannot begin to imagine all that you do for this world, Grandmother. All living creatures count on you and what the seasons bring. That you are able to make time for me amid all that is not something I'll ever take for granted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I thank you for the offering, though I am usually left cold. From mortals lately, merely begging, cries for mercy, nothing more. Let my crops grow, Lady Demeter. Save my harvest, Lady Demeter! Insufferable...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Where I am from, we lacked for plenty, but we never truly lacked. And I know that's in small part because of you, Grandmother. My headmistress thinks so highly of you, and I could see why from the moment we met like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps I ought have far more gratitude, myself. I think so often of my pain and lack... how could I not? Yet I have you, this mountain home, and more. Would only that the burden of it all ever lightened.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A gift of Ambrosia for you, Grandmother! Perhaps this can liven things up, for there ought to be much cause for celebration in the coming times. But mostly I just wanted you to have it, for all the hope you gave me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You say I gave you hope? I was intent on fighting the Titan no matter what. But having met you... I started to consider the possibility that we might prevail. Then I could see my daughter's gentle face, and her daughter's. I can think of no worthier cause.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Grandmother, you've stood by me ever since we met, and I felt our bond from the start. May this Ambrosia commemorate not just our past but our future. For I feel I still have so much to learn about you, and learn from you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall accept this, but no more. Know that your mother and I always had our struggles, flower. It was only once she lived her own life rather than my idea of it that our withered connection started to heal. May your and my relationship grow quickly past that point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: As the sapling struggles to grow, so too must we push ourselves to discover our truest potential. Behold my power over life and death; may you find yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The seasons are a never-ending cycle of decay and renewal... though there are such times when the seasons assert themselves much more fiercely than usual; times such as now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: How shameful that we now resort to common subterfuge, having our correspondence smuggled to and fro by the erstwhile Boatman of the Dead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The Titan's coin that you exchanged for this message... we shall find all of it, melt it down, and return the Gold whence it came.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: If only you could see my fields in the morning sun... shining brighter than any Gold you shall ever find. Such metals ought remain within the Earth.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: To be reduced to picking through such meager offerings! You ought have lived here in the glory of Olympus, where everything that you desire is at hand... for the most part.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, you're hurt! Your partly-mortal blood's to blame. And so am I, for having fallen into love with a mere farm-boy once...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: What happened, Granddaughter? May those wretches that hurt you thus suffer a thousandfold for what they have done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Injured already...! Know that the pains we suffer are the means by which we grow. And our enemies are about to grow a great deal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You spill your blood each night for our mistakes. I cannot cure your ailments from here, but I can make your enemies shatter to pieces, if that is of any comfort.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I am not angry with you any longer, Granddaughter; merely a little disappointed at the moment...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I cannot remain upset with you, flower. I would rather you look upon me kindly than with scorn.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sigh> My mood's as foul as the weather as of late... apologies for the outburst, flower.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You weathered the storm, though could have avoided it outright, of course...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: There need not be a strain between us, Granddaughter. Forgive me. I was rash.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: This family, and our blasted pride! These constant fights with our own kin! Feh.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother used to anger me in just this way, and look where that got her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us move on. Your provocation needn't have roused my ire to the extent that it did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: No harm comes of a little fight. We ought to focus on the real fight, instead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I am not angry with you, Granddaughter; and I admit I acted out of turn.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Melinoë, we mustn't fight! Not with each other, only side by side.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, I ought bury these impulses that drive me to act out of temper, not reason.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The weavers of destiny have sprouted up from the Earth once again! And conveniently right after we quelled the Titan's uprising without them having to intervene. We never could depend on the Fates...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: When my sisters and I and your father and his brothers first slew Chronos long ago, it was an ugly victory, but ushered in an age in which Olympus ruled. Now it seems another age is nigh... and I have yet to see a perfect one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I must admit that mortalkind unduly suffered in all this... so many prayers for reprieve left unheeded. But what else ought we to have done? Even the Fates themselves could not step in... dared not, perhaps.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: At times I fear that I've grown cold and bitter, but this matter has been quite rejuvenating in a way... ever since meeting you. I had perhaps been dormant for too long.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: What have you done? The Titan Chronos ought have been destroyed, not taken in. The rot is in his very soul and cannot be removed! But... my daughter, she sent word that she is well, and... oh, what am I to do with all of this...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Granddaughter... what would I have ever done if not for you. That this mountain yet stands is one thing, but then... you found your mother for the both of us. I cannot repay you... what love I have to give shall have to suffice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall never forgive Chronos, nor do I wish to see his wretched countenance again. But if whatever spell you cast on him has truly withered his ambitions, then so be it. Perhaps there was no other way...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You would spurn your own grandmother? Perhaps where you were raised, proper manners were not taught as a priority.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Even amid such times, we gods and goddesses yet have our pride; you would be wiser next time not to hurt mine thus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You are a direct descendant of mine, and still you would spurn me like this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Such wrath I feel, Melinoë, that I have a great deal to spare, even for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Had you grown up within your father's House, you would have learned respect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Understand that lately, I lack the patience for such antics, Granddaughter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not choose against me even in jest, Granddaughter; Apollo makes light, not I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: So what if Hestia is technically my elder? She hardly gives Olympus her attention!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You would forsake my blessing, even knowing it is sought after all the world over?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I take no pride in what I am about to do, Melinoë. But how else shall you learn?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: If this was your attempt at humor, know that it was lost on me entirely.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: If you are wondering whether that icy chill you feel in the air is mine, it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: As you begin your next attempt at the seemingly impossible, know that I shall be with you to the bitter end. In blessing form, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Go forth on this next ordeal with my blessing, Granddaughter. I've a feeling it shall be a very cold night for our enemies...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Should this night take you all the way to hell and back, I still would gladly come along. But, this is the best that I can do from here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The forecast for this evening calls for a sharp decline in temperature. Though you are well-accustomed to the cold by now, aren't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: How I would wait for your mother to rise from out of that Underworld and return to our mountaintop, as you are doing now. You and she are much alike in some respects, but she always had such a fiery temper....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Good. Turn your attention toward the Titan Chronos, or whatever foul remains of him as yet exist down in the Underworld depths. I long to see the age when we eliminate them all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: I thank you, flower, for you've thrown our enemies upon this mountain into turmoil. Even now they attempt to regroup, but with your continued effort, we may drive them back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Granddaughter, where have you been? In the deepest reaches of your father's realm, perhaps! Striking at the very heart of our enemy. One can but hope.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Aid from Olympus can take many different forms... as not all of us are suitably prepared or even built to face significant ordeals. Take, for instance, fairest Aphrodite over here...

dialogueLines.speaker: AphroditeUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Lady Demeter, you know that's patently untrue! I may be more a lover than a fighter, but that doesn't mean I won't mangle the faces of our foes, if that is what it takes to get them to back off! Now why don't we show your granddaughter how well we get along?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, flower, how mortals seek to be like us? Yet in so doing, they take on our ignoble qualities. Perhaps it's Lord Apollo's fault, as the standard to which they aspire!

dialogueLines.speaker: ApolloUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Did I offend somehow, good Lady Demeter? I know the times have been quite cruel, though why don't we direct that cruelty at our enemies, instead of heaping it upon each other here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Ares. Not even our most-eager god of war can put an immediate stop to the troubles that our family is having to weather. Have you so little control of your domain?

dialogueLines.speaker: AresUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: We all but do our best, O Demeter. For my part, I ensure that wars are instigated when required, and that necessary outcomes are achieved. But I cannot precisely guarantee their duration or intensity, for violence is a force unto itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Even I could use a bit of heat on some occasions, flower, which the forge of Hephaestus here provides. Perhaps as compensation for the utter lack of warmth between most members of our family.

dialogueLines.speaker: HephaestusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Ah, just listen to the Lady Demeter! Everybody on our mountaintop thinks they're so sharp, but this one? She is blunt like my hammer. And some jobs call for a blunt instrument, don't they?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Of late, even my radiant young sister must sully her delicate hands in all the field work that must be done. She is even beginning to take to it... aren't you, Hera our Queen?

dialogueLines.speaker: HeraUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Demeter. These hands have seen more than you know! I simply do not flaunt the hardships I've endured. One can be as strong as a rock, without taking on its texture.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Your Great-Aunt Hestia is not exactly as she seems. In olden times, she was content merely to let her flames keep sputtering within the hearths of mortal homes. Admit to it, Sister: You've changed.

dialogueLines.speaker: HestiaUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: And you, Demeter, you've not changed at all! None of you have! It's why you're in a heap of trouble now, or we I ought to say. Good thing your grandkid's coming to our rescue!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: The goddess of the hearth and I, together we have been through quite a bit... but not in quite some time. So what then, Hestia? Are you emboldened to rejoin us on Olympus on a more-permanent basis now?

dialogueLines.speaker: HestiaUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: How about we see the present matter to a proper close, and then I'll weigh that possibility, Sister! You lot can be a little much in case you haven't noticed, so I've not decided yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: Granddaughter, Poseidon here has been regaling me about the time his seas drank half the land! Ahh, such a flood that was! Perhaps our strengths can be combined again...

dialogueLines.speaker: PoseidonUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh, there's no perhaps about it, Demeter! We'll weather this storm yet! I'd just as soon whip up another great big flood for old Time's sake, it's just... too many mortals these days!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_01
dialogueLines.text: When my sisters and my foster-brothers and I overthrew Chronos long ago, we were much closer to your age, flower. Now we are older, and one should think wiser... although our frequent bickering and inaction suggest otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: ZeusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: We are a strong-willed family, as you know perfectly well, Lady Demeter. We must move forward in unison! Building that sort of consensus is not always quick; though we each could do our part to hasten it along.
